item.title: Building a Multi-Agent Document Intelligence System with Kiro CLI
item.description:
I needed a way to ask questions about a pile of documents — PDFs, Word files, images — and get a cited answer back. Not a summary of one file, but a synthesis across dozens of them. The kind of thing where you’d normally spend an afternoon reading, cross-referencing, and taking notes.

The problem is that LLMs choke when you try to stuff 50 documents into a single context window. So I built a multi-agent system where specialized workers each handle one file, and an orchestrator stitches the results together. Here’s how it works and why the architecture decisions matter.

item.title: Using AI to Write Blog Posts About Using AI
item.description:
I just published a blog post about refactoring this website with AI. The post itself was also written with AI. And then proof-read with AI. Here’s how that worked — the meta layer of using AI to document AI-assisted work.

The Problem

I’d spent a session refactoring the site’s UI with an AI pair programmer. The work was spread across 20+ commits, planning documents, design specs, and a running log of discoveries. Turning all of that into a coherent blog post meant synthesizing context from multiple sources, maintaining a consistent narrative, and not forgetting the interesting parts buried in the middle of the session.

item.title: Refactoring My Website with AI
item.description:
I rebuilt the visual design of this website using AI as a pair programmer. Not a one-shot “generate me a website” prompt — an iterative back-and-forth where I directed the design decisions and the AI handled the implementation. Here’s what happened, what worked, and what didn’t.

The Problem

This site started as a terminal-themed Hugo blog with a macOS window chrome aesthetic — traffic light dots, a rounded container floating on a dark background, per-page command prompts like me@abrahamsustaita.com:~$ cat post.md. It looked like a screenshot of a terminal rather than feeling like one.

item.title: WezTerm Configuration 1: Projects Selector
item.description:
In this post I’ll add my configuration for WezTerm. At the end, it will look like this:

Setting up keybindings

The first step is to add a key binding. In our case, it will be LEADER + p where LEADER will be mapped to CTRL + a.

In your .wezterm.lua file add:

 1local wezterm = require("wezterm")
 2local config = wezterm.config_builder()
 3
 4config.leader = { key = "a", mods = "CTRL", timeout_milliseconds = 2000 }
 5config.keys = {
 6 {
 7 mods = "LEADER",
 8 key = "p",
 9 action = wezterm.action_callback(function(window, pane)
10 end),
11 },
12}

With this configuration, now you’ll be able to type CTRL + a then p and it will… not do nothing.

item.title: WezTerm Configuration 2: UI
item.description:
In order to configure the WezTerm to have a nicer style, create the file ui.lua and update your wezterm.lua file like this:

1-- ... the previous Configuration
2local ui = require("ui")
3
4ui.set_ui(config)
5ui.set_smart_splits(config)

And in the ui.lua file add:

 1local wezterm = require("wezterm")
 2
 3---@class WezModule
 4local M = {}
 5
 6---@type number|nil
 7M.last_tab = nil
 8
 9---@param tab_info table
 10---@return string
 11local function get_tab_title(tab_info)
 12 local title = tab_info.tab_title
 13 return (title and #title > 0) and title or tab_info.active_pane.title
 14end
 15
 16---@param window table
 17local function create_workspace_status(window)
 18 local status = {
 19 background = { Background = { Color = "rgba(35, 33, 54, 0.95)" } },
 20 arrow = { Foreground = { Color = "rgba(35, 33, 54, 0.95)" } },
 21 }
 22
 23 if window:leader_is_active() then
 24 status.background = { Background = { Color = "#eb6f92" } }
 25 end
 26
 27 if window:active_tab():tab_id() ~= 0 then
 28 status.arrow = { Foreground = { Color = "#1e2030" } }
 29 end
 30
 31 return status
 32end
 33
 34---@param battery_state string
 35---@return string
 36local function get_battery_icon(battery_state)
 37 local icons = {
 38 ["AC attached"] = "🔌",
 39 ["Full"] = "🔌",
 40 ["Unknown"] = "🔌",
 41 ["Finishing charge"] = "🔌",
 42 ["Charging"] = "🔌",
 43 ["Discharging"] = "🔋",
 44 ["Battery Warning"] = "🪫",
 45 }
 46 return icons[battery_state] or "🔋"
 47end
 48
 49---@param time_seconds number
 50---@return string
 51local function format_time_remaining(time_seconds)
 52 if time_seconds <= 0 then
 53 return ""
 54 end
 55 local hours = math.floor(time_seconds / 3600)
 56 local minutes = math.floor((time_seconds % 3600) / 60)
 57 return string.format(" ⏳%02d:%02d", hours, minutes)
 58end
 59
 60---@param battery table
 61---@return string
 62local function format_battery_info(battery)
 63 local icon = get_battery_icon(battery.state)
 64 local percentage = string.format("%.0f%%", battery.state_of_charge * 100)
 65 local time_remaining = 0
 66
 67 if battery.state == "Discharging" then
 68 time_remaining = battery.time_to_empty or 0
 69 elseif battery.state == "Charging" then
 70 time_remaining = battery.time_to_full or 0
 71 end
 72
 73 return string.format(" %s%s%s", icon, percentage, format_time_remaining(time_remaining))
 74end
 75
 76---@class StyleColors
 77---@field edge string
 78---@field background string
 79---@field foreground string
 80
 81---@param text string
 82---@param colors StyleColors
 83---@return table[]
 84local function create_styled_section(text, colors)
 85 return {
 86 { Background = { Color = colors.edge } },
 87 { Foreground = { Color = colors.background } },
 88 { Text = "" },
 89 { Background = { Color = colors.background } },
 90 { Foreground = { Color = colors.foreground } },
 91 { Text = text },
 92 { Background = { Color = colors.edge } },
 93 { Foreground = { Color = colors.background } },
 94 { Text = "" },
 95 }
 96end
 97
 98---@param window table
 99local function update_right_status(window)
100 local colors = {
101 edge = "rgba(35, 33, 54, 0.95)",
102 background = "#9ccfd8",
103 foreground = "#2e3440",
104 }
105
106 local date = wezterm.strftime("📅 %b, %d ")
107 local time = wezterm.strftime("🕓 %H:%M ")
108 local battery = ""
109
110 for _, b in ipairs(wezterm.battery_info()) do
111 battery = battery .. format_battery_info(b)
112 end
113
114 local components = { { Text = "" } }
115 local sections = { date, time, battery }
116
117 for i, section in ipairs(sections) do
118 for _, item in ipairs(create_styled_section(section, colors)) do
119 table.insert(components, item)
120 end
121
122 if i < #sections then
123 table.insert(components, { Text = " " })
124 table.insert(components, { Text = "" })
125 end
126 end
127
128 window:set_right_status(wezterm.format(components))
129end
130
131---@param tab table
132---@param max_width number
133---@return table
134local function format_tab(tab, max_width)
135 local colors = {
136 edge = "rgba(35, 33, 54, 0.95)",
137 background = tab.is_active and "#ebbcba" or "#232136",
138 foreground = tab.is_active and "#2e3440" or "#d8dee9",
139 }
140
141 local icon = tab.is_active and "💻" or "🖥️"
142 local title = wezterm.truncate_right(get_tab_title(tab), max_width - 2)
143
144 return create_styled_section(icon .. " " .. title, colors)
145end
146---@param config table
147function M.set_ui(config)
148 wezterm.on("update-status", function(window, _)
149 local status = create_workspace_status(window)
150 local workspace_text = " " .. window:active_workspace() .. " "
151
152 window:set_left_status(wezterm.format({
153 status.background,
154 { Text = workspace_text },
155 status.arrow,
156 { Text = "" },
157 }))
158
159 update_right_status(window)
160 end)
161
162 wezterm.on("format-tab-title", function(tab, _, _, _, _, max_width)
163 return format_tab(tab, max_width)
164 end)
165
166 return config
167end
168
169---@param pane table
170---@return boolean
171local function is_vim(pane)
172 return pane:get_user_vars().IS_NVIM == "true"
173end
174
175---@param key string
176---@return string
177local function get_direction(key)
178 local directions = { h = "Left", j = "Down", k = "Up", l = "Right" }
179 return directions[key]
180end
181
182---@param resize_or_move "resize"|"move"
183---@param key string
184---@return table
185local function create_split_nav(resize_or_move, key)
186 local mods = resize_or_move == "resize" and "META" or "CTRL"
187
188 return {
189 key = key,
190 mods = mods,
191 action = wezterm.action_callback(function(win, pane)
192 if is_vim(pane) then
193 win:perform_action({
194 SendKey = { key = key, mods = mods },
195 }, pane)
196 else
197 local action = resize_or_move == "resize" and { AdjustPaneSize = { get_direction(key), 3 } }
198 or { ActivatePaneDirection = get_direction(key) }
199 win:perform_action(action, pane)
200 end
201 end),
202 }
203end
204
205---@param config table
206function M.set_smart_splits(config)
207 local keys = { "h", "j", "k", "l" }
208 for _, key in ipairs(keys) do
209 table.insert(config.keys, create_split_nav("move", key))
210 table.insert(config.keys, create_split_nav("resize", key))
211 end
212end
213
214return M

Privacy Policy

Effective Date: March 7, 2026
Last Updated: March 7, 2026

Introduction

This Privacy Policy describes how abrahamsustaita.com (“we,” “us,” or “our”) collects, uses, discloses, and protects information when you visit our website (the “Site”). This policy applies to all visitors and users of the Site.

By accessing or using the Site, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree with this policy, please do not use the Site.
